jd_full_text: Employee Experience Specialist — Coherent Corp. — Santa Clara, CA (onsite; ~25% travel). Compensation: $102,000-$138,000. NOTE: The Oracle Recruiting Cloud Candidate Experience page (https://hcwp.fa.us2.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX_1/job/2012324) is a JavaScript-rendered single-page app and could not be retrieved by automated fetch; the following is assembled from the verified role brief, not a verbatim copy. ROLE SUMMARY: The Employee Experience Specialist designs and runs the programs that shape how employees experience Coherent across the full lifecycle. This is a corporate HR / People role (no ITAR or export-control requirement). The role partners with HR and business leaders to build engagement, recognition, wellness, and culture programs grounded in employee data. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES: Design, launch, and run employee engagement, recognition, and wellness programs; own employee lifecycle programs including onboarding and offboarding; lead DEIB (Diversity, Equity, Inclusion & Belonging) and culture initiatives; develop and execute internal communications that reinforce culture and engagement; analyze employee survey and pulse engagement data and translate findings into action; partner cross-functionally with HR business partners and business leaders to deliver people programs. REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Communications, Psychology, Organizational Development, or a related field; 3-5 years of experience in employee experience, HR, people operations, engagement, or internal communications; experience designing and running people/culture programs and analyzing engagement data; strong cross-functional collaboration and communication skills. OTHER: Location Santa Clara, CA (onsite); Travel approximately 25%; Salary range $102,000-$138,000; no explicit 'no sponsorship' statement and no ITAR/export-control clause identified in the role summary.
fit: GO — Corporate EX/engagement role (no ITAR); Jamie's NextGen 2,000+ engagement-survey work, DEI pilots, and lifecycle/onboarding experience map directly to the engagement/recognition/wellness/DEIB/internal-comms scope.
